all.en: Eight friends gathered at a popular Italian restaurant to celebrate Hsin-yi's promotion.
all.zh: 八位朋友聚在一家很受歡迎的義大利餐廳，慶祝欣怡升職。

all.en: They ordered pasta, pizza, salads, and several bottles of wine throughout the evening.
all.zh: 整個晚上他們點了義大利麵、披薩、沙拉，還有好幾瓶紅酒。

all.en: Laughter and stories filled the table until the waiter finally brought the bill.
all.zh: 笑聲和故事填滿了餐桌，直到服務生終於送上帳單。

all.en: The total came to nearly twelve thousand dollars, much more than anyone had expected.
all.zh: 總金額將近一萬兩千元，比任何人預期的都要高出許多。

all.en: Some friends suggested dividing it equally for the sake of simplicity.
all.zh: 有些朋友建議為了簡單起見大家平均分攤。

all.en: However, two members hadn't eaten much and only drank water all night.
all.zh: 然而，有兩個人沒怎麼吃東西，整晚只喝水。

all.en: Hsin-yi quickly opened a bill-splitting app on her phone to help solve the problem.
all.zh: 欣怡很快地打開手機上的拆帳應用程式來幫忙解決這個問題。

all.en: She entered each person's order and the app calculated everyone's share fairly.
all.zh: 她輸入每個人的點餐，應用程式公平地算出了每個人應付的金額。

all.en: The wine drinkers paid a bit more, while the lighter eaters paid less.
all.zh: 喝酒的人多付一些，而吃得少的人付得少。

all.en: Everyone agreed that this method was much fairer than the old way.
all.zh: 每個人都同意這個方法比舊方式公平多了。

all.en: They left the restaurant happy, full, and without any awkwardness about money.
all.zh: 他們離開餐廳時心情愉快、肚子飽飽，也沒有任何關於金錢的尷尬。

all.en: On the walk home, Hsin-yi thanked everyone for celebrating her promotion so warmly.
all.zh: 在走路回家的路上，欣怡感謝大家如此熱情地慶祝她的升職。

all.en: A few friends suggested continuing the night at a quiet karaoke bar nearby.
all.zh: 幾位朋友提議到附近一間安靜的卡拉 OK 酒吧續攤。

all.en: They split a taxi into two groups since not everyone wanted to keep singing.
all.zh: 由於不是每個人都想繼續唱歌，他們分成兩組叫了計程車。

all.en: At the karaoke room, the group ordered light snacks and shared one fruit platter.
all.zh: 在卡拉 OK 包廂裡，這群人點了一些輕食並共享一盤水果拼盤。

all.en: This time, they decided in advance to split the bill exactly down the middle.
all.zh: 這次他們事先決定要把帳單從中對半平分。

all.en: Hsin-yi sang her favorite ballad while everyone clapped and recorded short videos.
all.zh: 欣怡唱了她最愛的抒情歌，大家鼓掌並錄下了短短的影片。

all.en: Around midnight, two friends called rideshare cars to head home together for safety.
all.zh: 接近午夜時，有兩位朋友共乘一輛網約車一起回家以策安全。

all.en: The remaining members chatted about future trips and possible shared vacation plans.
all.zh: 留下來的成員們聊著未來的旅行和可能的共同度假計畫。

all.en: Before saying goodbye, they took a group photo in front of the bright neon sign.
all.zh: 道別之前，他們在明亮的霓虹招牌前拍了一張團體合照。

all.en: Hsin-yi went to bed feeling thankful for friends who handled money so respectfully.
all.zh: 欣怡上床睡覺時，內心充滿了對於這些處理金錢如此體貼的朋友的感激。

all.en: The next morning, Hsin-yi sent a thank-you message to everyone who had attended the celebration.
all.zh: 隔天早上，欣怡傳了一則感謝訊息給每一位參加慶祝會的人。

all.en: She attached the group photo and a screenshot of the fair bill-splitting calculation results.
all.zh: 她附上了團體合照和那張公平拆帳計算結果的截圖。

all.en: Several friends replied with funny stickers and praised the app for saving the awkward moment.
all.zh: 好幾位朋友以有趣的貼圖回覆，並稱讚那款應用程式化解了尷尬的時刻。

all.en: One coworker asked her to recommend the same app for an upcoming team-building dinner.
all.zh: 一位同事請她推薦同一款應用程式，給即將到來的團隊建設晚餐使用。

all.en: Hsin-yi wrote a short blog post explaining how to split bills fairly among friends.
all.zh: 欣怡寫了一篇簡短的部落格文章，說明如何在朋友之間公平地拆帳。

all.en: The article received hundreds of comments from readers sharing their own awkward dining experiences.
all.zh: 那篇文章收到了數百則讀者留言，分享他們自己尷尬的用餐經驗。

all.en: A young couple confessed that money disputes had once damaged their oldest friendship circle.
all.zh: 一對年輕情侶坦承，金錢糾紛曾經破壞了他們最久遠的朋友圈。

all.en: Inspired by the feedback, Hsin-yi organized a monthly potluck where each guest brought one dish.
all.zh: 受到這些回饋的啟發，欣怡組織了月度的百樂餐聚，每位來賓帶一道菜。

all.en: This format completely removed money discussions and allowed everyone to enjoy each other's cooking.
all.zh: 這種形式完全消除了金錢方面的討論，讓每個人都能享受彼此的料理。

all.en: Her friend group grew closer because conversations focused on stories rather than splitting receipts.
all.zh: 她的朋友圈變得更親密，因為話題的重點是故事，而不是分攤收據。

all.en: After three successful potluck gatherings, Hsin-yi noticed that everyone arrived earlier and stayed much longer.
all.zh: 在三次成功的百樂餐聚之後，欣怡注意到大家都來得更早，也待得更久。

all.en: One friend confessed that he finally felt comfortable inviting his shy girlfriend to join the group.
all.zh: 一位朋友坦承，他終於敢自在地邀請他害羞的女友加入這個團體。

all.en: Another friend suggested rotating the host's home each month to share the cleanup work fairly.
all.zh: 另一位朋友建議每個月輪流到不同人家中作東，以便公平地分擔清理工作。

all.en: Hsin-yi created a simple shared online document to track who would bring which dish next time.
all.zh: 欣怡製作了一份簡單的線上共享文件，用來記錄下次誰要帶哪道菜。

all.en: The group started a fun tradition of teaching each other one family recipe at every gathering.
all.zh: 這個團體開始了一項有趣的傳統，在每次聚會中互相教導一道家傳食譜。

all.en: A local cooking magazine heard about their format and asked Hsin-yi for a short interview piece.
all.zh: 一家在地的烹飪雜誌聽說了她們的模式，邀請欣怡進行一篇簡短的專訪。

all.en: Her interview encouraged hundreds of readers to try similar bill-free gatherings within their own friend circles.
all.zh: 她的專訪鼓勵了數百位讀者在自己的朋友圈中嘗試類似免拆帳的聚會。

all.en: Hsin-yi happily replied to messages from strangers asking detailed questions about hosting potluck parties politely.
all.zh: 欣怡開心地回覆陌生人來信，回答他們關於得體舉辦百樂餐聚的詳細問題。

all.en: Eventually, she compiled the best tips into a free downloadable booklet she shared on her blog.
all.zh: 後來，她把最棒的建議整理成一本免費下載的小冊子，發布在她的部落格上。

all.en: Her once-strained friendships had truly transformed into a warm community built around food and trust.
all.zh: 她那些曾經緊繃的友誼，真正轉變成一個圍繞著美食與信任建立起來的溫暖社群。

all.en: After her booklet went viral, Hsin-yi received invitations to speak at company team-building events around Taipei.
all.zh: 在她的小冊子爆紅之後，欣怡收到了在台北各地公司團隊建設活動中演講的邀請。

all.en: Her first corporate workshop focused on how fair money habits could strengthen workplace friendships and team trust.
all.zh: 她的第一場企業工作坊聚焦於公平的金錢習慣如何能強化職場友誼與團隊信任。

all.en: Managers were surprised to learn that small dinner disputes often caused deeper conflicts within their teams.
all.zh: 主管們驚訝地得知，小小的晚餐糾紛常常在團隊中引發更深層的衝突。

all.en: Hsin-yi shared simple rules about pre-agreeing on budgets before any group meal or weekend trip.
all.zh: 欣怡分享了在任何團體聚餐或週末旅行之前先預先講好預算的簡單原則。

all.en: One company invited her back monthly to facilitate honest financial conversations between younger and older employees.
all.zh: 一家公司每月邀請她回去，協助年輕與資深員工之間進行誠實的財務對話。

all.en: She gradually built a small consulting business while still keeping her original day job at the firm.
all.zh: 她在保留原本公司日間工作的同時，逐漸建立了一份小型的顧問事業。

all.en: Her potluck group remained the heart of everything because it reminded her why connection truly mattered most.
all.zh: 她的百樂餐聚社群始終是一切的核心，因為它提醒著她連結真正最重要的原因。

all.en: On her thirtieth birthday, fifty friends gathered to celebrate with dishes representing every potluck memory together.
all.zh: 在她三十歲生日當天，五十位朋友帶著代表每一次百樂餐聚回憶的菜餚一同慶祝。

all.en: Hsin-yi cried happy tears, realizing how much one fair restaurant bill had truly changed her entire life.
all.zh: 欣怡流下了開心的眼淚，體認到一張公平的餐廳帳單真的徹底改變了她整個人生。

all.en: She wrote her next blog post about how small fairness can quietly grow into lasting community bonds.
all.zh: 她在下一篇部落格文章寫到，小小的公平如何能悄悄成長為持久的社區情感。

all.en: Hsin-yi began mentoring a younger colleague who struggled silently with rising credit card debt.
all.zh: 欣怡開始輔導一位年輕同事，他正默默承受著日益增加的卡債壓力。

all.en: They met every Wednesday at a small noodle stand far from the company's busy main office.
all.zh: 他們每週三在離公司熱鬧總部很遠的一間小麵攤碰面。

all.en: Over warm broth, she gently helped him list every honest debt and the smallest realistic monthly payment.
all.zh: 在暖暖的湯麵前，她溫柔地協助他列出每一筆誠實的欠款，以及最切實的最低月還款。

all.en: Within six months, he proudly closed his first credit card and treated her to a quiet meal.
all.zh: 六個月內，他驕傲地剪掉了第一張信用卡，並請她吃了一頓安靜的晚餐。

all.en: Together they split the bill fairly, including a small tip for the elderly noodle stand owner.
all.zh: 他們公平地分攤了帳單，還包括了給年長麵攤老闆的一筆小費。

all.en: The owner smiled and quietly added two free dumplings to each of their bowls every week afterward.
all.zh: 從此之後，那位老闆每週都靜靜地在他們的碗裡多放兩顆免費水餃。

all.en: Hsin-yi started a monthly small office circle for anyone wanting to discuss money respectfully and quietly.
all.zh: 欣怡開始在公司每月舉辦一個小型聚會，給願意安靜尊重地討論金錢的人。

all.en: Six people came to the first meeting, sharing tender stories over simple homemade rice ball lunches.
all.zh: 第一場聚會有六位同事出席，在簡單的自製飯糰午餐前分享了動人的故事。

all.en: Her younger colleague led the second meeting himself, with steady confidence she had quietly nurtured.
all.zh: 她那位年輕同事自己主持了第二場聚會，展現出她默默培育起的穩定自信。

all.en: A fair dinner bill had quietly grown into careers, friendships and quiet rescues across an entire workplace.
all.zh: 一張公平的晚餐帳單，已悄悄成長為整個職場中的職涯、友誼和靜默的救贖。

all.en: Word spread to another department, and three new members joined the monthly money circle next week.
all.zh: 消息傳到另一個部門，下週又有三位新成員加入了每月的金錢聚會。

all.en: Hsin-yi prepared a printed welcome sheet describing the group's simple guidelines for respectful and private sharing.
all.zh: 欣怡準備了一張印好的歡迎單，說明社群中關於尊重與保密分享的簡單守則。

all.en: She emphasized that no one needed to reveal exact salary numbers to feel safe and supported.
all.zh: 她強調沒有人需要透露確切的薪水數字，也能感到安心並獲得支持。

all.en: A senior engineer admitted he had hidden gambling losses from his wife for almost two years.
all.zh: 一位資深工程師坦承他將賭博虧損瞞著太太將近兩年了。

all.en: The group listened without judgment and helped him draft a calm, honest conversation plan for that weekend.
all.zh: 大家不帶批判地傾聽，幫他擬出了一份冷靜誠實的週末對話計畫。

all.en: On Monday, he returned looking lighter and shared that his wife had cried but forgiven him.
all.zh: 週一他回來時看起來輕鬆許多，分享說他太太雖然哭了但原諒了他。

all.en: Hsin-yi realized her quiet circles were saving marriages alongside savings accounts across the whole company.
all.zh: 欣怡體認到，她的安靜聚會在拯救存款帳戶的同時也在拯救整間公司裡的婚姻。

all.en: She decided to write a second booklet focused entirely on honest money talks between couples and families.
all.zh: 她決定撰寫第二本小冊子，完全聚焦在伴侶與家人之間誠實的金錢對話。

all.en: Her younger colleague offered to design the cover, choosing warm colors that felt safe and inviting.
all.zh: 她那位年輕同事自願設計封面，選用了讓人感到安心又溫暖的色調。

all.en: Together, they printed five hundred copies and quietly left them in cafés around the entire city.
all.zh: 他們一起印製了五百本，悄悄放在整座城市的咖啡館裡。
